title: subscribe_events panics on non-ASCII UTF-8 in SSE buffer: scan_from non-char-boundary
description:
In client.rs subscribe_events(), after appending a new chunk to the buffer, scan_from is set to old_len.saturating_sub(3) — a raw byte offset. If the buffer contains multibyte UTF-8 sequences (e.g. emoji U+1F600 is 4 bytes, euro sign U+20AC is 3 bytes) and old_len-3 falls inside such a sequence, then &buf[scan_from..] panics with 'byte index N is not a char boundary'.

Trigger: any SSE payload that contains non-ASCII characters (emoji in a StateChange data payload, Unicode in a presence statusText, CJK characters anywhere). This is virtually certain in production for an international chat application.

Root cause: scan_from was introduced as an optimization in R2 (jmap-chat-tui-xbt.9) to avoid O(n^2) rescanning. The optimization uses byte arithmetic on a String buffer without ensuring char boundary alignment.

Reproduction sketch:
  let buf = "abc\u{20AC}x"; // 7 bytes: a(1)+b(1)+c(1)+€(3)+x(1)
  let old_len = buf.len(); // 7
  let scan_from = old_len.saturating_sub(3); // 4
  let _ = &buf[scan_from..]; // PANIC: byte index 4 is not a char boundary

Fix options:
  (a) Iterate backward from old_len.saturating_sub(3) until a char boundary is found:
      let scan_from = (0..=old_len.saturating_sub(3)).rev()
          .find(|&i| buf.is_char_boundary(i)).unwrap_or(0);
  (b) Use Bytes (Vec<u8>) as the buffer type and search byte-level patterns directly
      (safe because all SSE delimiters \r\n, \n\n, \r\r are ASCII, which cannot
      appear as interior bytes of a multibyte UTF-8 sequence).
  Option (b) is preferred: it eliminates the class of problem and avoids the
  repeated UTF-8 validation cost.
